claim_boundary: this matrix keeps current runtime dense, hull-cache, and sparse-top-k execution evidence separate from research-only hierarchical-hull and proxy generic-attention baselines; the proxy rows are cost-model comparisons on the same article-class workload artifact, not promoted runtime or served capability claims
summary: Public efficient-attention baseline matrix now freezes 6 article-class workload rows under one shared artifact contract: current promoted HullCache is fastest on 2 workloads, the research hierarchical-hull candidate is fastest on 4, and the generic linear/recurrent proxy is fastest on 0; sparse, reformer-style, and hull-family rows now carry explicit win/tie/lose/refuse posture against the dense reference floor instead of comparing only to naive dense headlines.
